projectDescription: Thesis is an agent-first research workspace that gives researchers and AI agents a shared, durable operating system for scientific work — a graph of hypotheses and results, an append-only research log, indexed knowledge sources, artifacts, skills, and approved compute.
keyFeatures: Research graph — a DAG of staged and committed nodes for hypotheses, experiments, and insights
Append-only research log for decisions, pivots, approvals, and telemetry
Unified knowledge search across the Thesis graph and external sources (papers, repos, docs, datasets)
Built-in agent harness with 27 project-aware tools and a ReAct loop
Human-approved compute — sub-agent runs require explicit approval before spending budget
86 MCP tools accessible from Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenCode, and other MCP hosts
CLI npm package (@synsci/thesis) for one-command MCP setup and skill installation
Six skill families covering autonomous research, graph ingestion, survey, reproduce, and lookahead
Artifact and filesystem layer for evidence, files, reports, and export packages
Subgraph export to JSON, markdown, and PDF for handoff and reproducibility
publicApiSurface: POST /mcp-server — JSON-RPC 2.0 endpoint for all 86 MCP tools
GET /api/agent/chat — SSE stream for agent chat sessions
thesis_stage_node_create — create a new staged research node
thesis_commit_node — commit a node to durable history
thesis_branch_node — create a child node from a parent
thesis_search — unified search across graph and external sources
thesis_index_paper / thesis_index_repo / thesis_index_docs / thesis_index_dataset — index external sources
thesis_oracle_start_job — queue a research synthesis job
thesis_compute_acquire / thesis_compute_release — manage GPU compute leases
thesis_request_compute_grant_approval — request human approval for compute spending
thesis_export_subgraph — export a subgraph as JSON
thesis_export_summary / thesis_export_summary_pdf — export markdown or PDF summaries
npx @synsci/thesis setup — CLI command to install MCP config and skills
npx @synsci/thesis uninstall — CLI command to remove MCP config
